Started antibiotics and albumin.", teachingPoint: "Always tap ascites - SBP presents subtly." } } }, // ==================== THYROID EMERGENCIES DEEP CASE ==================== 'thyroid-deep': { id: 'thyroid-deep', title: 'Thyroid Emergencies', subtitle: 'Thyroid storm and myxedema coma', category: 'Endocrinology', difficulty: 'Advanced', estimatedTime: '50-65 min', tags: ['Thyroid Storm', 'Myxedema', 'PTU', 'Beta-Blocker'], primer: { anatomy: { title: "Thyroid Physiology", sections: [ { title: "HPT Axis", content: "The thyroid axis regulates metabolism:", keyPoints: [ "Hypothalamus → TRH → Pituitary → TSH → Thyroid → T4/T3", "T4 is the prohormone, T3 is active", "TSH is the most sensitive marker of thyroid function" ], clinicalCorrelation: "Treatment blocks synthesis, release, and peripheral conversion." }, { title: "Thyroid Storm", content: "Life-threatening hyperthyroidism exacerbation:", keyPoints: [ "Precipitants: Infection, surgery, stopping medications", "Burch-Wartofsky score ≥45 suggests storm", "Mortality 10-30% with treatment" ], clinicalCorrelation: "Diagnosis is clinical - don't wait for labs." } ] }, physiology: { title: "Treatment", sections: [ { title: "Thyroid Storm Treatment Order", content: "Sequence matters:", keyPoints: [ "1. Beta-blocker (propranolol) - immediate symptom control", "2. PTU - blocks synthesis AND T4→T3 conversion", "3. Iodine (1 hour after PTU) - blocks release", "4. Steroids - blocks conversion, treats adrenal insufficiency" ], clinicalCorrelation: "Iodine before PTU can worsen hyperthyroidism (Jod-Basedow)." } ], quiz: [ { question: "What's the correct treatment order for thyroid storm?", options: ["Iodine → PTU → Beta-blocker", "Beta-blocker → PTU → Iodine → Steroids", "PTU → Iodine → Beta-blocker", "Steroids first"], correctIndex: 1, explanation: "Beta-blocker first for symptoms, PTU blocks synthesis, iodine 1h later blocks release, steroids block conversion." } ] } }, longitudinalTimeline: [ { day: 1, title: "Emergency Department", setting: "ED", timeLabel: "Hour 0" }, { day: 2, title: "ICU Day 1", setting: "ICU", timeLabel: "Day 1" } ], day1: { patientInfo: { name: "Sandra Peterson", age: 42, gender: "female", firstName: "Sandra", lastName: "Peterson", setting: "hospital_inpatient", chiefComplaint: "Racing heart, fever, confusion" }, vitals: { BP: "158/72", HR: 148, RR: 24, Temp: "103.2°F", SpO2: "97% RA" }, hpiSections: [ { question: "What happened?", answer: "Graves' disease, stopped methimazole 3 weeks ago. Agitated, sweating, pacing for 2 days.", mechanismLink: { title: "Why these symptoms?", explanation: "Thyroid hormone increases metabolic rate and catecholamine sensitivity." } } ], physicalExam: { general: "Agitated, diaphoretic", HEENT: "Lid lag, proptosis, goiter", cardiovascular: "Irregularly irregular (AFib), tachycardic" }, initialLabs: { thyroid: { TSH: "<0.01", freeT4: 5.8, freeT3: 18.2 }, labInterpretation: [{ lab: "TSH suppressed, T4/T3 elevated", meaning: "Confirms severe thyrotoxicosis" }] }, initialManagement: { title: "Thyroid Storm Management", interventions: [ { action: "Propranolol 1mg IV then 60-80mg PO q4h", rationale: "First - controls symptoms immediately" }, { action: "PTU 600mg load, then 200mg q4h", rationale: "Blocks synthesis and conversion" }, { action: "SSKI 5 drops q6h - 1 HOUR AFTER PTU", rationale: "Blocks hormone release" }, { action: "Hydrocortisone 100mg IV q8h", rationale: "Blocks conversion, treats relative AI" } ] }, dayConclusion: { summary: "42-year-old with thyroid storm (Burch-Wartofsky 90). Started sequential therapy.", teachingPoint: "Treatment order matters: beta-blocker → PTU → iodine (1h later) → steroids." } } }, // ==================== BACTERIAL MENINGITIS DEEP CASE ==================== 'meningitis-deep': { id: 'meningitis-deep', title: 'Bacterial Meningitis', subtitle: 'CSF analysis, empiric therapy, and dexamethasone', category: 'Infectious Disease', difficulty: 'Advanced', estimatedTime: '50-65 min', tags: ['CNS Infection', 'LP', 'CSF', 'Antibiotics', 'Dexamethasone'], primer: { anatomy: { title: "Meningeal Anatomy", sections: [ { title: "Meninges and CSF", content: "The meninges protect the CNS:", keyPoints: [ "Three layers: Dura, arachnoid, pia mater", "CSF in subarachnoid space", "Blood-brain barrier limits antibiotic penetration" ], clinicalCorrelation: "Inflammation increases BBB permeability, improving antibiotic penetration." }, { title: "Pathogens by Age", content: "Empiric therapy is age-based:", keyPoints: [ "Adults <50: S. pneumoniae, N. meningitidis", "Adults ≥50: Add Listeria coverage (ampicillin)", "Post-neurosurgery: MRSA and Pseudomonas coverage" ], clinicalCorrelation: "Listeria is resistant to cephalosporins - must add ampicillin if ≥50 or immunocompromised." } ] }, physiology: { title: "Diagnosis and Treatment", sections: [ { title: "CSF Findings", content: "CSF analysis distinguishes bacterial from viral:", keyPoints: [ "Bacterial: WBC >1000 (neutrophils), glucose low (<40), protein high", "Viral: WBC <500 (lymphocytes), glucose normal", "Gram stain positive in 60-90% of untreated bacterial" ], clinicalCorrelation: "Treat based on clinical suspicion - don't wait for CSF if delayed." }, { title: "Empiric Antibiotics", content: "Don't delay treatment:", keyPoints: [ "Under 50: Ceftriaxone + Vancomycin", "50 or older: ADD Ampicillin", "Dexamethasone BEFORE or WITH first antibiotic dose" ], clinicalCorrelation: "Every hour of antibiotic delay increases mortality ~7%." } ], quiz: [ { question: "68-year-old with fever and confusion - empiric regimen?", options: ["Ceftriaxone alone", "Ceftriaxone + Vancomycin", "Ceftriaxone + Vancomycin + Ampicillin", "Ampicillin alone"], correctIndex: 2, explanation: "Age ≥50 requires ampicillin for Listeria coverage. Full regimen: Ceftriaxone + Vancomycin + Ampicillin." } ] } }, longitudinalTimeline: [ { day: 1, title: "Emergency Department", setting: "ED", timeLabel: "Hour 0" }, { day: 2, title: "ICU Day 1", setting: "ICU", timeLabel: "Day 1" } ], day1: { patientInfo: { name: "Alex Kumar", age: 22, gender: "male", firstName: "Alex", lastName: "Kumar", setting: "emergency_department", chiefComplaint: "Severe headache, fever, stiff neck" }, vitals: { BP: "108/68", HR: 112, RR: 20, Temp: "102.8°F", SpO2: "98% RA" }, hpiSections: [ { question: "Symptom onset?", answer: "College student, worst headache of life overnight, then fever and confusion. Lives in dormitory.", mechanismLink: { title: "Why neck stiffness?", explanation: "Meningeal inflammation causes painful resistance to neck flexion." } } ], physicalExam: { general: "Ill, photophobic", neck: "Severe nuchal rigidity, positive Kernig and Brudzinski signs", skin: "Petechiae on lower extremities" }, initialLabs: { CSF: { WBC: 2400, neutrophils: "92%", glucose: 18, protein: 380, gramStain: "Gram-negative diplococci" }, labInterpretation: [{ lab: "Gram-negative diplococci", meaning: "N. meningitidis - meningococcal meningitis" }] }, initialManagement: { title: "Meningitis Management", interventions: [ { action: "Dexamethasone 0.15mg/kg IV BEFORE antibiotics", rationale: "Reduces inflammation and improves outcomes" }, { action: "Ceftriaxone 2g IV + Vancomycin 1g IV within 15 min", rationale: "Empiric coverage - young adult doesn't need ampicillin" }, { action: "Droplet precautions, notify public health", rationale: "Meningococcal disease is contagious and reportable" } ] }, dayConclusion: { summary: "22-year-old with meningococcal meningitis. Dex before antibiotics.", teachingPoint: "Don't delay antibiotics. Dexamethasone before or with first dose." } } }, // ==================== ANAPHYLAXIS DEEP CASE ==================== 'anaphylaxis-deep': { id: 'anaphylaxis-deep', title: 'Anaphylaxis', subtitle: 'Recognition, epinephrine, and biphasic reactions', category: 'Allergy/Immunology', difficulty: 'Intermediate', estimatedTime: '40-50 min', tags: ['Emergency', 'Epinephrine', 'Airway', 'Biphasic'], primer: { anatomy: { title: "Anaphylaxis Pathophysiology", sections: [ { title: "IgE-Mediated Reaction", content: "Mast cell degranulation causes multi-system effects:", keyPoints: [ "Allergen crosslinks IgE on mast cells → immediate degranulation", "Histamine causes vasodilation, bronchoconstriction, urticaria", "Common triggers: Foods, insect stings, medications" ], clinicalCorrelation: "Symptoms develop within minutes. Faster onset often means more severe reaction." } ] }, physiology: { title: "Treatment", sections: [ { title: "Epinephrine is First Line", content: "Epinephrine is the ONLY first-line treatment:", keyPoints: [ "Dose: 0.3-0.5mg IM anterolateral thigh", "Repeat every 5-15 min if needed", "NO contraindications in anaphylaxis", "Antihistamines/steroids are adjunctive only" ], clinicalCorrelation: "Delayed epinephrine is the most common error. Give it FIRST." }, { title: "Biphasic Reactions", content: "Symptoms can recur hours later:", keyPoints: [ "Occurs in 1-20% of cases, usually 8-12 hours", "Observe minimum 4-6 hours", "Always discharge with epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen) prescription" ], clinicalCorrelation: "Never discharge without epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen) and allergy referral." } ], quiz: [ { question: "Patient with throat tightness and BP 78/50 after eating shrimp - first treatment?", options: ["Diphenhydramine", "Methylprednisolone", "Epinephrine IM", "Albuterol"], correctIndex: 2, explanation: "Epinephrine IM is ALWAYS first for anaphylaxis." } ] } }, longitudinalTimeline: [ { day: 1, title: "Emergency Department", setting: "ED", timeLabel: "Hour 0" }, { day: 2, title: "Observation", setting: "ED", timeLabel: "Hour 6" } ], day1: { patientInfo: { name: "Teresa Nguyen", age: 34, gender: "female", firstName: "Teresa", lastName: "Nguyen", setting: "emergency_department", chiefComplaint: "Difficulty breathing after eating" }, vitals: { BP: "82/54", HR: 124, RR: 28, Temp: "98.6°F", SpO2: "91% RA" }, hpiSections: [ { question: "What happened?", answer: "Peanut allergy, ate Thai food 20 min ago, developed lip tingling, hives, throat closing, nearly passed out.", mechanismLink: { title: "Why so rapid?", explanation: "IgE-mediated reactions cause immediate mast cell degranulation." } } ], physicalExam: { general: "Anxious, using accessory muscles", HEENT: "Lip/tongue angioedema, stridor", skin: "Diffuse urticaria" }, initialManagement: { title: "Anaphylaxis Management", interventions: [ { action: "Epinephrine 0.5mg IM IMMEDIATELY", rationale: "Only first-line treatment" }, { action: "High-flow O2, prepare for airway", rationale: "Stridor indicates upper airway involvement" }, { action: "Large-bore IV, NS 2L bolus", rationale: "Distributive shock from vasodilation" }, { action: "Diphenhydramine + famotidine + methylprednisolone", rationale: "Adjunctive - NOT substitutes for epi" } ] }, dayConclusion: { summary: "Anaphylaxis from peanut exposure, required 2 doses epinephrine.", teachingPoint: "Epinephrine first. No contraindications. Repeat if needed. Always discharge with epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen)." } } }, // ==================== ADRENAL INSUFFICIENCY DEEP CASE ==================== 'adrenal-deep': { id: 'adrenal-deep', title: 'Adrenal Insufficiency', subtitle: 'Primary vs secondary, stress dosing, adrenal crisis', category: 'Endocrinology', difficulty: 'Intermediate', estimatedTime: '45-55 min', tags: ['Cortisol', 'HPA Axis', 'Stress Dosing', 'Crisis'], primer: { anatomy: { title: "HPA Axis", sections: [ { title: "Primary vs Secondary AI", content: "The distinction affects presentation:", keyPoints: [ "Primary (Addison's): Adrenal destruction → low cortisol AND aldosterone", "- Features: Hyperpigmentation, hyperkalemia, salt craving", "Secondary: Pituitary/steroid suppression → low cortisol, aldosterone preserved", "- Features: No hyperpigmentation, no hyperkalemia", "Most common cause: Chronic steroid use" ], clinicalCorrelation: "Primary has hyperkalemia (no aldosterone). Secondary does not." } ] }, physiology: { title: "Management", sections: [ { title: "Adrenal Crisis", content: "Medical emergency requiring immediate treatment:", keyPoints: [ "Precipitants: Infection, stopping steroids, surgery", "Presentation: Refractory hypotension, hypoglycemia", "Treatment: Hydrocortisone 100mg IV + fluids", "Response is often dramatic" ], clinicalCorrelation: "Don't wait for test results - treat empirically." }, { title: "Stress Dosing", content: "Cortisol needs increase during stress:", keyPoints: [ "Minor illness: Double the dose", "Severe illness/surgery: Hydrocortisone 50-100mg q8h", "Never abruptly stop steroids" ], clinicalCorrelation: "Patients must understand sick day rules." } ], quiz: [ { question: "Addison's patient with BP 76/48, glucose 52, ran out of hydrocortisone. Treatment?", options: ["Fludrocortisone", "Dexamethasone", "Hydrocortisone 100mg IV + NS + D50", "Cosyntropin test first"], correctIndex: 2, explanation: "Adrenal crisis: Hydrocortisone 100mg IV (has mineralocorticoid activity), fluids, glucose. Don't delay for testing." } ] } }, longitudinalTimeline: [ { day: 1, title: "Emergency Department", setting: "ED", timeLabel: "Hour 0" }, { day: 2, title: "Hospital Day 1", setting: "ICU", timeLabel: "Day 1" } ], day1: { patientInfo: { name: "Michael Rodriguez", age: 52, gender: "female", firstName: "Michael", lastName: "Rodriguez", setting: "emergency_department", chiefComplaint: "Weakness, nausea, dizziness" }, vitals: { BP: "78/52", HR: 108, RR: 18, Temp: "100.2°F", SpO2: "97% RA" }, hpiSections: [ { question: "What happened?", answer: "On prednisone for lupus, tapered to 5mg. Got sick 2 weeks ago and hasn't recovered. Vomiting, can't eat.", mechanismLink: { title: "Why crisis now?", explanation: "HPA axis suppressed from prior high-dose steroids. Couldn't increase cortisol in response to illness." } } ], physicalExam: { general: "Ill, lethargic", skin: "Hyperpigmentation on knuckles", cardiovascular: "Hypotensive, orthostatic" }, initialLabs: { BMP: { Na: 126, K: 5.8, Glucose: 58 }, other: { cortisol: 2.1 }, labInterpretation: [{ lab: "Cortisol 2.1, Na 126, K 5.8", meaning: "Adrenal insufficiency with classic electrolyte pattern" }] }, initialManagement: { title: "Adrenal Crisis Management", interventions: [ { action: "Hydrocortisone 100mg IV immediately", rationale: "Life-saving - don't wait for tests" }, { action: "NS bolus 2L, D50 for hypoglycemia", rationale: "Volume and glucose replacement" }, { action: "Continue hydrocortisone 50mg IV q6h", rationale: "Stress dosing until stable" } ] }, dayConclusion: { summary: "52-year-old with adrenal crisis from steroid suppression. Dramatic response to hydrocortisone.", teachingPoint: "In suspected adrenal crisis, give hydrocortisone empirically. Response confirms diagnosis." } } } }; // State for deep reasoning mode let deepReasoningState = { currentCase: null, currentDay: 1, primerComplete: false, primerSection: 'anatomy', // anatomy, physiology, quiz quizAnswers: {}, reasoningResponses: {}, mechanismPanelOpen: false, currentMechanism: null, timelinePosition: 0 }; // ==================== MAIN DEEP REASONING RENDERER ==================== function renderDeepReasoningCase() { const caseData = deepReasoningState.currentCase; if (!caseData) return renderDeepReasoningMenu(); // Determine what to show based on progress if (!deepReasoningState.primerComplete) { // Handle different primer property names (sciencePrimer vs primer) const primer = caseData.sciencePrimer || caseData.primer; if (primer) { // Check if this is a DKA-style primer (with sections) or pancreatitis-style (with content) if (caseData.id === 'dka-deep') { return renderDKAPrimer(primer); } return renderSciencePrimer(primer); } } // Show longitudinal case based on current day return renderLongitudinalDay(caseData, deepReasoningState.currentDay); } // ==================== DEEP REASONING MENU ==================== function renderDeepReasoningMenu() { return `

🔬 Deep Reasoning Cases

Go beyond pattern recognition. Understand why diseases present the way they do, how tests detect pathology, and why treatments work.

🧬

Science Foundation

Review anatomy & physiology before the case

🔗

Mechanism Links

Connect every finding to pathophysiology

📅

Longitudinal Care

Follow patients from ED to follow-up

Available Deep Cases

📣 Request a Topic

Is there a clinical topic you'd like to see as a Deep Reasoning case? We're building cases based on student feedback and curriculum needs.

`; } function startDeepReasoningCase(caseId) { deepReasoningState = { currentCase: deepReasoningCases[caseId], currentDay: 1, primerComplete: false, primerSection: 'anatomy', quizAnswers: {}, reasoningResponses: {}, mechanismPanelOpen: false, currentMechanism: null, timelinePosition: 0 }; state.screen = 'deep-reasoning'; render(); } // ==================== SCIENCE PRIMER RENDERER ==================== function renderSciencePrimer(primer) { const section = deepReasoningState.primerSection; const sectionData = primer[section]; return `

🧬 ${primer.title}

Build your foundation before seeing the patient

${section === 'anatomy' ? renderAnatomySection(primer.anatomy) : ''} ${section === 'physiology' ? renderPhysiologySection(primer.physiology) : ''} ${section === 'quiz' ? renderPrimerQuiz(primer) : ''}
`; } function renderAnatomySection(anatomy) { return `

${anatomy.title}

Objectives: ${anatomy.objectives.join(' • ')}
${anatomy.externalResources ? `

🔗 External Anatomy Resources UBC Clinical Anatomy

Explore these interactive resources for deeper anatomical understanding. Opens in new tab.

${anatomy.externalResources.map(res => ` ${res.type === 'interactive-atlas' ? '🗺️' : ''} ${res.type === 'interactive-module' ? '📚' : ''} ${res.type === '3d-model' ? '🧊' : ''} ${res.type === 'radiology' ? '🩻' : ''}
${res.title}
${res.description}
↗ `).join('')}

Source: clinicalanatomy.ca (Creative Commons BY-NC-SA 4.0)

` : ''}
${anatomy.content.map(item => `

${item.title}

${item.text}

💡 Clinical Correlation

${item.clinicalCorrelation}

`).join('')}
`; } function renderPhysiologySection(physiology) { return `

${physiology.title}

Objectives: ${physiology.objectives.join(' • ')}
${physiology.externalResources ? `

🔗 External Physiology Resources UBC Clinical Anatomy

Explore enzyme function and digestive physiology. Opens in new tab.

${physiology.externalResources.map(res => ` 📚
${res.title}
${res.description}
↗ `).join('')}

Source: clinicalanatomy.ca (Creative Commons BY-NC-SA 4.0)

` : ''}
${physiology.content.map(item => `

${item.title}

${item.text}

💡 Clinical Correlation

${item.clinicalCorrelation}

`).join('')}
`; } function renderPrimerQuiz(primer) { const allQuestions = [ ...primer.anatomy.interactiveQuiz.map(q => ({...q, source: 'anatomy'})), ...primer.physiology.interactiveQuiz.map(q => ({...q, source: 'physiology'})) ]; const allAnswered = allQuestions.every((q, i) => deepReasoningState.quizAnswers[`primer_${i}`] !== undefined); const allCorrect = allQuestions.every((q, i) => { const answer = deepReasoningState.quizAnswers[`primer_${i}`]; return answer !== undefined && q.options[answer].correct; }); return `

Check Your Understanding

Before seeing the patient, make sure you understand the foundations.

${allQuestions.map((q, idx) => { const answered = deepReasoningState.quizAnswers[`primer_${idx}`] !== undefined; const selectedIdx = deepReasoningState.quizAnswers[`primer_${idx}`]; return `
Question ${idx + 1} • ${q.source === 'anatomy' ? 'Anatomy' : 'Physiology'}

${q.question}

${q.options.map((opt, optIdx) => { let btnClass = 'border border-zinc-200 dark:border-zinc-700'; if (answered) { if (opt.correct) { btnClass = 'border-2 border-green-500 bg-green-50 dark:bg-green-900/20'; } else if (selectedIdx === optIdx && !opt.correct) { btnClass = 'border-2 border-red-500 bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-900/20'; } } return ` `; }).join('')}
${answered ? `

${q.explanation}

` : ''}
`; }).join('')}
${allAnswered ? `
${allCorrect ? '🎉' : '📚'}

${allCorrect ? 'Excellent! You\'re ready.' : 'Good effort! Review and continue.'}

${allCorrect ? 'You understand the foundations. Time to apply this knowledge to a real patient.' : 'You can review the anatomy and physiology sections anytime. Let\'s see how it applies clinically.'}

` : `

Answer all questions to continue

`} `; } // ==================== DKA PRIMER RENDERER ==================== // DKA case uses a different structure (sections + quiz instead of content + interactiveQuiz) function renderDKAPrimer(primer) { const section = deepReasoningState.primerSection; const sectionData = primer[section]; return `

🩸 DKA: Science Foundation

Understand the metabolic pathways before managing the emergency

${section === 'anatomy' ? renderDKAAnatomySection(sectionData) : ''} ${section === 'physiology' ? renderDKAPhysiologySection(sectionData) : ''} ${section === 'quiz' ? renderDKAPrimerQuiz(primer) : ''}
`; } function renderDKAAnatomySection(anatomy) { return `

${anatomy.title}

${anatomy.externalResources ? `

🔗 External Resources

${anatomy.externalResources.map(res => ` 📚
${res.title}
${res.description}
↗ `).join('')}
` : ''}
${anatomy.sections.map(section => `

${section.title}

${section.content}

${section.keyPoints ? `
    ${section.keyPoints.map(point => `
  • ${point}
    • `).join('')}
` : ''} ${section.clinicalCorrelation ? `
💡 Clinical Correlation

${section.clinicalCorrelation}

` : ''}
`).join('')}
`; } function renderDKAPhysiologySection(physiology) { return `

${physiology.title}

${physiology.sections.map(section => `

${section.title}

${section.content}

${section.keyPoints ? `
    ${section.keyPoints.map(point => `
  • ${point}
    • `).join('')}
` : ''} ${section.clinicalCorrelation ? `
💡 Clinical Correlation

${section.clinicalCorrelation}

` : ''}
`).join('')}
`; } function renderDKAPrimerQuiz(primer) { // DKA quiz questions are in primer.physiology.quiz const allQuestions = primer.physiology.quiz || []; const allAnswered = allQuestions.every((q, i) => deepReasoningState.quizAnswers[`primer_${i}`] !== undefined); const allCorrect = allQuestions.every((q, i) => { const answer = deepReasoningState.quizAnswers[`primer_${i}`]; if (answer === undefined) return false; return q.options[answer].correct; }); return `

🧪 Check Your Understanding

Apply what you learned about DKA pathophysiology.

${allQuestions.map((q, qIdx) => { const answered = deepReasoningState.quizAnswers[`primer_${qIdx}`] !== undefined; const selectedIdx = deepReasoningState.quizAnswers[`primer_${qIdx}`]; return `

${qIdx + 1}. ${q.question}

${q.options.map((opt, optIdx) => { let btnClass = 'w-full p-3 text-left border rounded-lg transition '; if (!answered) { btnClass += 'border-zinc-200 dark:border-zinc-700 hover:border-amber-500'; } else if (optIdx === selectedIdx) { btnClass += opt.correct ? 'border-green-500 bg-green-50 dark:bg-green-900/20' : 'border-red-500 bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-900/20'; } else if (opt.correct && answered) { btnClass += 'border-green-500 bg-green-50 dark:bg-green-900/20'; } else { btnClass += 'border-zinc-200 dark:border-zinc-700 opacity-50'; } return ` `; }).join('')}
${answered && q.explanation ? `
Explanation: ${q.explanation}
` : ''}
`; }).join('')}
${allAnswered ? `
${allCorrect ? '🎉' : '📚'}

${allCorrect ? 'Excellent! You understand DKA pathophysiology.' : 'Good effort! Review the concepts and continue.'}

${allCorrect ? 'Time to apply this knowledge to manage a real DKA patient.' : 'The case will reinforce these concepts. Let\'s see how it applies clinically.'}

` : `

Answer all questions to continue

`} `; } function answerDKAPrimerQuiz(qIdx, optIdx) { deepReasoningState.quizAnswers[`primer_${qIdx}`] = optIdx; render(); } // ==================== LONGITUDINAL DAY RENDERER ==================== function renderLongitudinalDay(caseData, dayNum) { const timeline = caseData.longitudinalTimeline; const dayKey = `day${dayNum}`; const dayData = caseData[dayKey]; if (!dayData) return '

Day data not found

'; return `
${timeline.map((t, idx) => { const isActive = t.day === dayNum; const isPast = t.day < dayNum; const isFuture = t.day > dayNum; return ` ${idx < timeline.length - 1 ? '→' : ''} `; }).join('')}

${dayData.title || timeline.find(t => t.day === dayNum).title}

${timeline.find(t => t.day === dayNum).timeLabel} • ${timeline.find(t => t.day === dayNum).setting}

${renderDayContent(dayData, dayNum)}
${renderMechanismPanel()}
${dayNum > 1 ? ` ` : '
'} ${dayNum < timeline.length ? ` ` : ` `}
`; } function renderDayContent(dayData, dayNum) { const caseId = deepReasoningState.currentCase?.id || ''; // Use a generic renderer that works for both cases based on data structure // Day 1 is always the initial presentation if (dayNum === 1) { return renderGenericDay1(dayData, caseId); } // For other days, try case-specific renderers first, then generic if (caseId === 'pancreatitis-deep') { if (dayNum === 3) return renderDay3Content(dayData); if (dayNum === 5) return renderDay5Content(dayData); if (dayNum === 7) return renderDay7Content(dayData); if (dayNum === 8) return renderDay8Content(dayData); if (dayNum === 30) return renderDay30Content(dayData); } if (caseId === 'dka-deep') { if (dayNum === 2) return renderDKAHour4(dayData); if (dayNum === 3) return renderDKAHour12(dayData); if (dayNum === 4) return renderDKAFloor(dayData); if (dayNum === 5) return renderDKADischarge(dayData); if (dayNum === 30) return renderDKAFollowup(dayData); } return '

Content not available for day ' + dayNum + ' in case ' + caseId + '

'; } // ==================== GENERIC DAY 1 RENDERER ==================== function renderGenericDay1(day, caseId) { let html = `

${day.patientInfo.name}, ${day.patientInfo.age}yo ${day.patientInfo.gender}

${day.patientInfo.setting ? `

${window.getSettingDisplayName ? window.getSettingDisplayName(day.patientInfo.setting) : day.patientInfo.setting}

` : ''}

"${day.patientInfo.chiefComplaint}"

Vital Signs
`; // Handle different vital sign formats if (day.vitals) { html += `BP ${day.vitals.BP} | HR ${day.vitals.HR} | RR ${day.vitals.RR}`; if (day.vitals.glucose) html += `
Glucose: ${day.vitals.glucose}`; } html += `
`; // HPI Sections (for both cases) if (day.hpiSections) { html += `

📋 History

${day.hpiSections.map(section => `
${section.question}

${section.answer}

${section.mechanismLink ? `
🔬 ${section.mechanismLink.title}
${section.mechanismLink.explanation}
` : ''}
`).join('')}
`; } // Physical Exam if (day.physicalExam) { html += `

🩺 Physical Exam

${day.physicalExam.general ? `
General: ${day.physicalExam.general}
` : ''} ${day.physicalExam.HEENT ? `
HEENT: ${day.physicalExam.HEENT}
` : ''} ${day.physicalExam.cardiovascular ? `
CV: ${day.physicalExam.cardiovascular}
` : ''} ${day.physicalExam.respiratory ? `
Resp: ${day.physicalExam.respiratory}
` : ''} ${day.physicalExam.abdomen ? `
Abd: ${day.physicalExam.abdomen}
` : ''} ${day.physicalExam.neuro ? `
Neuro: ${day.physicalExam.neuro}
` : ''}
`; } // Exam Findings with explanations (DKA style) if (day.examFindings) { html += `

Key Clinical Findings

${day.examFindings.map(f => `
${f.finding}
${f.explanation}
`).join('')}
`; } // Labs if (day.labResults) { html += `

🔬 ${day.labResults.title || 'Laboratory Results'}

${day.labResults.values.map(lab => `
${lab.test} ${lab.value} ${lab.normal ? `(${lab.normal})` : ''} ${lab.mechanism ? `
${lab.mechanism}
` : ''}
`).join('')}
`; } // Severity Assessment (DKA) if (day.severityAssessment) { html += `

📊 ${day.severityAssessment.title}

${day.severityAssessment.overall}
`; } // Initial Management if (day.initialManagement) { html += `

💊 ${day.initialManagement.title}

${day.initialManagement.priority ? `

Priority: ${day.initialManagement.priority}

` : ''}
${day.initialManagement.steps.map(step => `
${step.order ? step.order + '. ' : ''}${step.action}
${step.specifics || step.details || ''}
${step.rationale ? `
Why?
${step.rationale}
` : ''} ${step.warning ? `
⚠️ ${step.warning}
` : ''}
`).join('')}
`; } // Clinical Decision if (day.clinicalDecision) { html += `

🤔 Clinical Decision

${day.clinicalDecision.question}

${day.clinicalDecision.options.map((opt, idx) => ` `).join('')}
`; } // SBAR Call if (day.sbarCall) { html += `
📞

Call ${day.sbarCall.attending}

${day.sbarCall.context}

`; } // Day Conclusion if (day.dayConclusion) { html += `

${day.dayConclusion.summary}

💡 Clinical Pearl: ${day.dayConclusion.clinicalPearl}
`; } return html; } // ==================== DKA SPECIFIC RENDERERS ==================== function renderDKAHour4(day) { return `

${day.title}

Current Vitals

BP ${day.currentStatus.vitals.BP} | HR ${day.currentStatus.vitals.HR}
UOP: ${day.currentStatus.vitals.UOP}

Clinical Status

${day.currentStatus.clinicalChange}

📊 ${day.intervalLabs.title}

${day.intervalLabs.values.map(lab => `
${lab.test} ${lab.initial} → ${lab.now} ${lab.status}
`).join('')}

🤔 ${day.managementCheck.title}

${day.managementCheck.questions.map(q => `
${q.question}
Answer: ${q.answer}
${q.rationale}
${q.commonMistake ? `
⚠️ Common mistake: ${q.commonMistake}
` : ''}
`).join('')}

⚠️ ${day.complicationWatch.title}

${day.complicationWatch.complications.map(c => `
${c.name}
Risk factors: ${c.riskFactors}
Signs: ${c.signs}
Prevention: ${c.prevention}
`).join('')}

${day.dayConclusion.summary}

💡 ${day.dayConclusion.clinicalPearl}
`; } function renderDKAHour12(day) { return `

${day.title}

${day.currentStatus.clinicalStatus}

✅ ${day.resolutionLabs.title}

${day.resolutionLabs.values.map(lab => `
${lab.test} ${lab.value} ${lab.status}
`).join('')}
${day.resolutionLabs.conclusion}

💉 ${day.transitionProtocol.title}

${day.transitionProtocol.steps.map(step => `
Step ${step.step}: ${step.action}
${step.detail}
${step.example ? `
${step.example}
` : ''}
`).join('')}

⚠️ Common Errors:

    ${day.transitionProtocol.commonErrors.map(e => `
  • • ${e}
    • `).join('')}

${day.dayConclusion.summary}

💡 ${day.dayConclusion.clinicalPearl}
`; } function renderDKAFloor(day) { return `

${day.title}

Glucose Log:

${day.currentStatus.glucoseLog.map(g => `
${g.time} ${g.value} ${g.insulin}
`).join('')}

📊 ${day.insulinAdjustment.title}

Current: ${day.insulinAdjustment.currentRegimen}

${day.insulinAdjustment.assessment.map(a => `
${a.issue}
${a.interpretation}
→ ${a.action}
`).join('')}

📚 ${day.diabetesEducation.title}

${day.diabetesEducation.sessions.map(s => `
${s.topic}
    ${s.keyPoints.map(p => `
  • • ${p}
    • `).join('')}
`).join('')}

${day.dayConclusion.summary}

💡 ${day.dayConclusion.clinicalPearl}
`; } function renderDKADischarge(day) { return `

✅ ${day.dischargeReadiness.title}

${day.dischargeReadiness.criteria.map(c => `
✓ ${c.item}
`).join('')}

💊 ${day.dischargeMedications.title}

${day.dischargeMedications.insulinRegimen.map(med => `
${med.name}
${med.dose}
${med.notes}
`).join('')}

📋 ${day.dischargeInstructions.title}

${day.dischargeInstructions.sections.map(s => `
${s.topic}
    ${s.instructions.map(i => `
  • • ${i}
    • `).join('')}
`).join('')}

📅 Follow-up

${day.followUp.appointments.map(a => `
${a.type}
${a.purpose}
${a.timing}
`).join('')}

${day.dayConclusion.summary}

💡 ${day.dayConclusion.clinicalPearl}
`; } function renderDKAFollowup(day) { return `

${day.title}

${day.setting}

${day.patientUpdate.subjective}

🔬 Labs

${day.labResults.values.map(lab => `
${lab.test} ${lab.value} ${lab.interpretation}
`).join('')}

🎉 ${day.alcoholCessation?.title || 'Progress'}

${day.preventionReview ? `
${day.preventionReview.discussion.map(d => `
${d.question}
${d.answer}
`).join('')}
` : ''}

🎓 Case Complete

${day.caseConclusion.summary}

Key Takeaways:

    ${day.caseConclusion.keyTakeaways.map(t => `
  • ✓ ${t}
    • `).join('')}
💎 Clinical Pearl:

${day.caseConclusion.clinicalPearl}

`; } // Generic clinical decision handler function selectGenericClinicalDecision(idx, caseId) { const caseData = deepReasoningState.currentCase; const dayNum = deepReasoningState.currentDay; const dayKey = 'day' + dayNum; const options = caseData[dayKey]?.clinicalDecision?.options; if (!options) return; const feedback = document.getElementById('generic-decision-feedback'); // Reset buttons options.forEach((_, i) => { const btn = document.getElementById('generic-decision-' + i); if (btn) btn.className = 'w-full p-3 border border-zinc-200 dark:border-zinc-700 rounded-lg text-left text-sm'; }); const btn = document.getElementById('generic-decision-' + idx); const opt = options[idx]; if (opt.correct) { btn.className = 'w-full p-3 border-2 border-green-500 bg-green-50 dark:bg-green-900/20 rounded-lg text-left text-sm'; feedback.className = 'mt-4 p-3 rounded-lg bg-green-50 dark:bg-green-900/20'; feedback.innerHTML = '✓ Correct! ' + opt.explanation; } else { btn.className = 'w-full p-3 border-2 border-red-500 bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-900/20 rounded-lg text-left text-sm'; feedback.className = 'mt-4 p-3 rounded-lg bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-900/20'; feedback.innerHTML = 'Not quite. ' + opt.explanation; } feedback.classList.remove('hidden'); } // Generic SBAR handler function startGenericSbarCall(caseId) { const caseData = deepReasoningState.currentCase; const dayNum = deepReasoningState.currentDay; const dayKey = 'day' + dayNum; const sbar = caseData[dayKey]?.sbarCall; if (!sbar) return; const modal = document.getElementById('generic-sbar-modal'); modal.innerHTML = `
🔴 Connected to ${sbar.attending}
`; modal.classList.remove('hidden'); } function submitGenericSbar(caseId) { const caseData = deepReasoningState.currentCase; const dayNum = deepReasoningState.currentDay; const dayKey = 'day' + dayNum; const sbar = caseData[dayKey]?.sbarCall; const s = document.getElementById('sbar-s').value; const b = document.getElementById('sbar-b').value; const a = document.getElementById('sbar-a').value; const r = document.getElementById('sbar-r').value; let response = sbar.attendingResponses.good; if (s.length > 50 && b.length > 50 && a.length > 50 && r.length > 30) { response = sbar.attendingResponses.excellent; } else if (s.length < 20 || b.length < 20) { response = sbar.attendingResponses.needsWork; } document.getElementById('generic-sbar-modal').classList.add('hidden'); const feedback = document.getElementById('generic-sbar-feedback'); feedback.innerHTML = `
📞 ${sbar.attending} responds:

"${response}"

View ideal SBAR
S: ${sbar.idealSBAR.situation}
B: ${sbar.idealSBAR.background}
A: ${sbar.idealSBAR.assessment}
R: ${sbar.idealSBAR.recommendation}
`; feedback.classList.remove('hidden'); } function renderDay3Content(day) { return `

Clinical Update

Updated Vitals
BP ${day.updatedVitals.BP} | HR ${day.updatedVitals.HR} | T ${day.updatedVitals.Temp}

${day.clinicalStatus}

⚠️ New Concern

New symptoms: ${day.newFindings.symptoms}

Exam: ${day.newFindings.exam}

Labs: WBC ${day.newFindings.labs.WBC}, Lipase ${day.newFindings.labs.lipase}

🔬 Complication: ${day.complication.name}

${day.complication.explanation.title}

Mechanism: ${day.complication.explanation.mechanism}

Management: ${day.complication.explanation.management}

Prognosis: ${day.complication.explanation.prognostic}

${day.sbarCall ? `
📞

Call the Attending

${day.sbarCall.context}

${day.sbarCall.imagingOptions.map(opt => ` `).join('')}

Now present this patient to ${day.sbarCall.attending} using SBAR format.

🔴 Connected to ${day.sbarCall.attending}

"${day.sbarCall.attending} speaking. What's going on?"

` : ''}

Clinical Decision

${day.clinicalDecision.question}

${day.clinicalDecision.options.map((opt, idx) => ` `).join('')}
`; } function renderDay5Content(day) { return `

📈 ${day.clinicalProgress.status}

Improvements:

    ${day.clinicalProgress.details.map(d => '
  • ✓ ' + d + '
    • ').join('')}

Labs:

WBC: ${day.clinicalProgress.labs.WBC}
Lipase: ${day.clinicalProgress.labs.lipase}
BUN: ${day.clinicalProgress.labs.BUN}

🔪 ${day.surgicalConsultation.title}

${day.surgicalConsultation.assessment}

${day.surgicalConsultation.criteriaForSurgery.title}

${day.surgicalConsultation.criteriaForSurgery.criteria.map(c => `
${c.met ? '✅' : '⏳'}
${c.criterion}
${c.explanation}
`).join('')}
Conclusion: ${day.surgicalConsultation.criteriaForSurgery.conclusion}

❓ ${day.surgicalConsultation.timingDiscussion.question}

${day.surgicalConsultation.timingDiscussion.answer}

    ${day.surgicalConsultation.timingDiscussion.evidence.map(e => '
  • • ' + e + '
    • ').join('')}
Exception: ${day.surgicalConsultation.timingDiscussion.caveat}

🍷 ${day.alcoholCounseling.title}

"${day.alcoholCounseling.discussion.opening}"

    ${day.alcoholCounseling.discussion.keyPoints.map(p => '
  • • ' + p + '
    • ').join('')}
Recommendation: ${day.alcoholCounseling.discussion.recommendation}

Day ${5} Summary

${day.dayConclusion.summary}

📚 Teaching Point: ${day.dayConclusion.teachingPoint}
`; } function renderDay7Content(day) { return `

🔪 ${day.surgeryNote.title}

Procedure: ${day.surgeryNote.procedure}
Duration: ${day.surgeryNote.duration}
EBL: ${day.surgeryNote.EBL}
Complications: ${day.surgeryNote.complications}
View operative findings
    ${day.surgeryNote.findings.map(f => '
  • • ' + f + '
    • ').join('')}

Post-Op Day 1 Assessment

Vitals

BP ${day.postOpDay1.vitals.BP} | HR ${day.postOpDay1.vitals.HR}
T ${day.postOpDay1.vitals.Temp} | SpO2 ${day.postOpDay1.vitals.SpO2}

Status

Pain: ${day.postOpDay1.assessment.pain}
Diet: ${day.postOpDay1.assessment.diet}
Activity: ${day.postOpDay1.assessment.activity}

⚠️ ${day.postOpComplications.title}

${day.postOpComplications.watchFor.map(c => `
${c.complication}
Presentation: ${c.presentation}
Timing: ${c.timing}
Management: ${c.management}
`).join('')}

✓ ${day.postOpComplications.reassurance}

${day.dischargeReadiness.title}

${day.dischargeReadiness.criteria.map(c => `
${c.met === true ? '✅' : c.met === 'partial' ? '⏳' : c.met === 'pending' ? '📝' : '❌'} ${c.criterion}: ${c.status}
`).join('')}
Plan: ${day.dischargeReadiness.plan}

${day.dayConclusion.summary}

📚 Teaching Point: ${day.dayConclusion.teachingPoint}
`; } function renderDay8Content(day) { return `

🏠 Ready for Discharge

${day.morningAssessment.status}
${day.morningAssessment.exam}
BP ${day.morningAssessment.vitals.BP}
HR ${day.morningAssessment.vitals.HR} | T ${day.morningAssessment.vitals.Temp}

✅ ${day.dischargeChecklist.title}

${day.dischargeChecklist.criteria.map(c => `
✓
${c.item}
${c.notes}
`).join('')}

📋 ${day.dischargeEducation.title}

${day.dischargeEducation.sections.map(s => `
${s.topic}
    ${s.instructions.map(i => '
  • • ' + i + '
    • ').join('')}
${s.emphasize ? '
⚠️ Important: ' + s.emphasize + '
' : ''}
`).join('')}

📅 Follow-up Appointments

${day.followUpPlan.appointments.map(a => `
${a.type}
${a.purpose}
${a.timing}
`).join('')}

❓ ${day.patientQuestions.title}

${day.patientQuestions.qa.map(qa => `
"${qa.q}"
${qa.a}
`).join('')}

${day.dayConclusion.summary}

📚 Teaching Point: ${day.dayConclusion.teachingPoint}
`; } function renderDay30Content(day) { return `

Patient Update - ${day.setting}

${day.patientUpdate.subjective}

Positives:

    ${day.patientUpdate.positives.map(p => '
  • ✓ ' + p + '
    • ').join('')}

Concerns:

    ${day.patientUpdate.concerns.map(c => '
  • • ' + c + '
    • ').join('')}

🔬 ${day.labResults.title}

${day.labResults.results.map(r => `
${r.test} ${r.value} ${r.interpretation}
`).join('')}

🩸 ${day.glucoseDiscussion.title}

Finding: ${day.glucoseDiscussion.finding}

Possible causes:

    ${day.glucoseDiscussion.possibleCauses.map(c => '
  • • ' + c + '
    • ').join('')}

Plan:

    ${day.glucoseDiscussion.plan.map(p => '
  • → ' + p + '
    • ').join('')}
📚 Teaching: ${day.glucoseDiscussion.teachingPoint}

🎉 ${day.alcoholCessation.title}

Status: ${day.alcoholCessation.status}

    ${day.alcoholCessation.details.map(d => '
  • ✓ ' + d + '
    • ').join('')}
Reinforcement: ${day.alcoholCessation.reinforcement}

📋 ${day.longTermPlan.title}

${day.longTermPlan.items.map(item => `
${item.issue}
${item.plan}
`).join('')}

🎓 Case Complete

${day.caseConclusion.summary}

Key Takeaways:

    ${day.caseConclusion.keyTakeaways.map(t => `
  • ✓ ${t}
    • `).join('')}
💎 Clinical Pearl:

${day.caseConclusion.clinicalPearl}

`; } // ==================== MECHANISM PANEL ==================== function renderMechanismPanel() { return `

🧬 Mechanism Explorer

Click any "Why?" or "🔬 Mechanism" link to explore the pathophysiology.

💡 Tip: Understanding mechanisms helps you remember clinical facts and apply knowledge to new situations.

📊 Your Progress

Mechanism links explored: 0
Reasoning prompts completed: 0
`; } // ==================== HELPER FUNCTIONS ==================== function setPrimerSection(section) { deepReasoningState.primerSection = section; render(); } function completePrimerSection(section) { deepReasoningState.quizAnswers[section + '_complete'] = true; } function answerPrimerQuiz(questionIdx, optionIdx) { deepReasoningState.quizAnswers[`primer_${questionIdx}`] = optionIdx; render(); } function completePrimer() { deepReasoningState.primerComplete = true; render(); } function goToLongitudinalDay(day) { deepReasoningState.currentDay = day; render(); } function advanceDeepReasoningDay() { // Get the timeline from the current case const caseData = deepReasoningState.currentCase; if (!caseData || !caseData.longitudinalTimeline) { deepReasoningState.currentDay++; render(); return; } // Find current position in timeline and advance to next const timeline = caseData.longitudinalTimeline; const currentIdx = timeline.findIndex(t => t.day === deepReasoningState.currentDay); if (currentIdx >= 0 && currentIdx < timeline.length - 1) { // Advance to the next day in timeline deepReasoningState.currentDay = timeline[currentIdx + 1].day; } render(); } function exitDeepReasoning() { state.screen = 'menu'; render(); } function toggleHistoryExpand(id) { const detail = document.getElementById(`history-detail-${id}`); const icon = document.getElementById(`expand-icon-${id}`); if (detail.classList.contains('hidden')) { detail.classList.remove('hidden'); icon.textContent = '−'; } else { detail.classList.add('hidden'); icon.textContent = '+'; } } function showLabMechanism(id) { const el = document.getElementById(`lab-mechanism-${id}`); el.classList.toggle('hidden'); } function showTreatmentMechanism(id) { const el = document.getElementById(`tx-mechanism-${id}`); el.classList.toggle('hidden'); } function toggleComplicationExplanation() { document.getElementById('complication-explanation').classList.toggle('hidden'); } function toggleEducationMechanism(topic) { document.getElementById(`edu-mechanism-${topic.replace(/\s+/g, '-')}`).classList.toggle('hidden'); } function toggleFollowupMechanism(topic) { document.getElementById(`followup-mechanism-${topic.replace(/\s+/g, '-')}`).classList.toggle('hidden'); } function showReflectionAnswer() { document.getElementById('reflection-answer').classList.toggle('hidden'); } function submitReasoningResponse(num) { const response = document.getElementById(`reasoning-response-${num}`).value; const feedback = document.getElementById(`reasoning-feedback-${num}`); feedback.classList.remove('hidden'); feedback.innerHTML = `
✓ Response recorded
Key concepts to include:
  • Acinar cell damage releases enzymes
  • Premature enzyme activation cascade
  • Cellular injury reflected in blood levels
`; } function selectClinicalDecision(idx) { const caseData = deepReasoningState.currentCase; const options = caseData.day3.clinicalDecision.options; const feedback = document.getElementById('decision-feedback'); // Reset all buttons options.forEach((_, i) => { document.getElementById(`decision-btn-${i}`).className = 'w-full p-3 border border-zinc-200 dark:border-zinc-700 rounded-lg text-left'; }); // Highlight selected const btn = document.getElementById(`decision-btn-${idx}`); const opt = options[idx]; if (opt.correct) { btn.className = 'w-full p-3 border-2 border-green-500 bg-green-50 dark:bg-green-900/20 rounded-lg text-left'; feedback.className = 'mt-4 p-3 rounded-lg bg-green-50 dark:bg-green-900/20'; feedback.innerHTML = `✓ Correct! ${opt.explanation}`; } else { btn.className = 'w-full p-3 border-2 border-red-500 bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-900/20 rounded-lg text-left'; feedback.className = 'mt-4 p-3 rounded-lg bg-red-50 dark:bg-red-900/20'; feedback.innerHTML = `Not quite. ${opt.explanation}`; } feedback.classList.remove('hidden'); } // ==================== SBAR CALL FUNCTIONS ==================== let selectedSbarImaging = null; function selectSbarImaging(imagingId) { const caseData = deepReasoningState.currentCase; const sbarCall = caseData.day3.sbarCall; const option = sbarCall.imagingOptions.find(o => o.id === imagingId); // Reset all buttons sbarCall.imagingOptions.forEach(opt => { const btn = document.getElementById(`sbar-img-${opt.id}`); if (btn) { btn.className = 'w-full p-3 border border-indigo-200 dark:border-indigo-700 rounded-lg text-left hover:border-indigo-400 transition bg-white dark:bg-zinc-800'; } }); // Highlight selected const selectedBtn = document.getElementById(`sbar-img-${imagingId}`); selectedSbarImaging = option; // Show feedback const feedbackDiv = document.getElementById('sbar-imaging-feedback'); if (option.appropriate) { selectedBtn.className = 'w-full p-3 border-2 border-green-500 bg-green-50 dark:bg-green-900/20 rounded-lg text-left'; feedbackDiv.className = 'mt-3 p-3 rounded-lg bg-green-50 dark:bg-green-900/20'; feedbackDiv.innerHTML = `✓ Good choice! ${option.feedback}`; } else { selectedBtn.className = 'w-full p-3 border-2 border-amber-500 bg-amber-50 dark:bg-amber-900/20 rounded-lg text-left'; feedbackDiv.className = 'mt-3 p-3 rounded-lg bg-amber-50 dark:bg-amber-900/20'; feedbackDiv.innerHTML = `Consider another option. ${option.feedback}`; } feedbackDiv.classList.remove('hidden'); // Show Step 2 (call button) after selection document.getElementById('sbar-step-2').classList.remove('hidden'); } function startSbarCall() { document.getElementById('sbar-step-1').classList.add('hidden'); document.getElementById('sbar-step-2').classList.add('hidden'); document.getElementById('sbar-call-modal').classList.remove('hidden'); // Pre-populate recommendation with selected imaging if (selectedSbarImaging) { document.getElementById('sbar-recommendation').value = `I would like to order ${selectedSbarImaging.name} to evaluate for pancreatic necrosis and assess the pleural effusions. Do you agree with this plan?`; } } function submitSbarCall() { const caseData = deepReasoningState.currentCase; const sbarCall = caseData.day3.sbarCall; const idealSBAR = sbarCall.idealSBAR; // Get student responses const situation = document.getElementById('sbar-situation').value.trim(); const background = document.getElementById('sbar-background').value.trim(); const assessment = document.getElementById('sbar-assessment').value.trim(); const recommendation = document.getElementById('sbar-recommendation').value.trim(); // Simple scoring based on key elements let score = 0; let feedback = []; // Check Situation const situationKeywords = ['48', 'pancreatitis', 'respiratory', 'dyspnea', 'shortness', 'breath', 'oxygen']; const situationHits = situationKeywords.filter(kw => situation.toLowerCase().includes(kw)).length; if (situationHits >= 2) { score++; feedback.push({ section: 'Situation', status: 'good', note: 'You identified the patient and reason for calling.' }); } else { feedback.push({ section: 'Situation', status: 'improve', note: 'Include patient identifiers, diagnosis, and why you are calling.' }); } // Check Background const bgKeywords = ['gallstone', 'alcohol', 'lipase', 'ct', 'fluids', 'admitted', 'day']; const bgHits = bgKeywords.filter(kw => background.toLowerCase().includes(kw)).length; if (bgHits >= 3) { score++; feedback.push({ section: 'Background', status: 'good', note: 'Good summary of relevant history and hospital course.' }); } else { feedback.push({ section: 'Background', status: 'improve', note: 'Include admission diagnosis, key findings, and treatment so far.' }); } // Check Assessment const assessKeywords = ['effusion', 'wbc', 'necrosis', 'worsening', 'concern', 'dyspnea', 'oxygen', 'decreased', 'breath']; const assessHits = assessKeywords.filter(kw => assessment.toLowerCase().includes(kw)).length; if (assessHits >= 3) { score++; feedback.push({ section: 'Assessment', status: 'good', note: 'Clear clinical assessment with your concerns.' }); } else { feedback.push({ section: 'Assessment', status: 'improve', note: 'State what is happening now, exam findings, labs, and your clinical impression.' }); } // Check Recommendation const recKeywords = ['ct', 'imaging', 'scan', 'recommend', 'order', 'contrast']; const recHits = recKeywords.filter(kw => recommendation.toLowerCase().includes(kw)).length; if (recHits >= 2 && selectedSbarImaging && selectedSbarImaging.appropriate) { score++; feedback.push({ section: 'Recommendation', status: 'good', note: 'Appropriate imaging recommendation with clear ask.' }); } else if (recHits >= 1) { feedback.push({ section: 'Recommendation', status: 'okay', note: 'You made a recommendation - make sure it is the optimal imaging choice and clearly stated.' }); } else { feedback.push({ section: 'Recommendation', status: 'improve', note: 'Always end with a specific recommendation and what you need from the attending.' }); } // Determine attending response let attendingResponse; if (score >= 4) { attendingResponse = sbarCall.attendingResponses.excellent; } else if (score >= 2) { attendingResponse = sbarCall.attendingResponses.good; } else { attendingResponse = sbarCall.attendingResponses.needsWork; } // Hide the call modal document.getElementById('sbar-call-modal').classList.add('hidden'); // Show feedback const feedbackDiv = document.getElementById('sbar-feedback'); feedbackDiv.innerHTML = `
📞 ${sbarCall.attending} responds:

"${attendingResponse}"

Your SBAR Feedback:

${feedback.map(f => `
${f.status === 'good' ? '✅' : f.status === 'okay' ? '⚠️' : '💡'}
${f.section}: ${f.note}
`).join('')}
View example SBAR for this scenario
S: ${idealSBAR.situation}
B: ${idealSBAR.background}
A: ${idealSBAR.assessment}
R: ${idealSBAR.recommendation}
`; feedbackDiv.classList.remove('hidden'); } function completeLongitudinalCase() { const caseName = deepReasoningState.currentCase?.title || 'this case'; alert(`Congratulations! You completed the Deep Reasoning case. Your understanding of the pathophysiology will help you apply this knowledge to future patients.`); exitDeepReasoning(); } // Store reference to the core submitRevisedDifferential function const _coreSubmitRevisedDifferential = submitRevisedDifferential; // Re-establish core function after all scripts load (protects from external overrides) window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() { // Check if the function was overwritten by external scripts const currentFn = window.submitRevisedDifferential; if (currentFn !== _coreSubmitRevisedDifferential) { // Create a wrapper that handles both small group mode AND standard mode const externalFn = currentFn; window.submitRevisedDifferential = function() { // If we're on the revised-differential screen (post-physical), use core function if (window.state && window.state.screen === 'revised-differential') { return _coreSubmitRevisedDifferential(); } // Otherwise, if external function exists, use it (e.g., small group mode on initial differential) if (typeof externalFn === 'function') { return externalFn(); } // Fallback to core return _coreSubmitRevisedDifferential(); }; } }); // ============================================================ // EXPOSE FUNCTIONS TO WINDOW FOR EXTERNAL SCRIPTS // Required for reasondx-patient-response-core.js integration // ============================================================ window.matchHistoryQuestion = matchHistoryQuestion; window.detectQuestionIntent = detectQuestionIntent; window.expandMedicalAbbreviations = expandMedicalAbbreviations;
